testTopology: tests/ holds 87 .csproj: 81 runnable test projects + 4 reusable TestKits + 1 shared harness (tests/Shared/Koan.Testing) + 1 fixture host (tests/Fixtures/Koan.Mcp.TestHost). Layout per tests/README.md: Suites/<Domain>/<Scope>, Shared/, SeedPacks/ (only 1 pack: core/default.json), plus 16 LOOSE legacy-style projects at tests/ root (Koan.AI.{Contracts.Shared,EndToEnd,Eval,Integration,Models,Prompt,Review}.Tests, Koan.Web.{Auth,Json.Strict,PatchOps,Sse}.Tests, Koan.WebSockets.Tests, Koan.ZenGarden.Tests, S16.PantryPal[.McpHost].Tests, S7.Meridian.Tests — ~79 cs files total). Suite counts (projects / non-bin cs files): AI 2/26, Cache 9/42, Canon 3/22, Context 1/12, Core 2/13, Data 21/158 (largest), Integration 1/14, Jobs 7/27, Media 1/53, Samples 3/21, Security 2/8, Storage 2/8, Tagging 1/5, Web 13/93. ~2,097 [Fact]/[Theory] attributes across 352 files. Stack: xunit 2.9.3 + xunit.runner.visualstudio 3.1.5 + AwesomeAssertions 9.4.0 + MS Test.Sdk 18.6.0 + TimeProvider.Testing; Testcontainers referenced by 13 projects. TestKit/oracle patterns: (1) FilterConvergence oracle (tests/Suites/Data/AdapterSurface/Koan.Data.AdapterSurface.TestKit/FilterConvergence.cs) — shared 5-widget corpus + operator×member-shape case battery cross-checked against InMemoryFilterEvaluator; divergence can only mean a wrong pushdown translation; per-adapter spec instantiations for Sqlite/Postgres/SqlServer/Mongo/Couchbase/InMemory/PGVector plus TemporalConvergence.cs (DATA-0100 comparable encoding); (2) VectorAdapterSurface TestKit (VectorFilterConvergenceSpecsBase) instantiated for Weaviate/Qdrant/Milvus/OpenSearch/ElasticSearch/InMemory; (3) Web.AdapterSurface.TestKit instantiated across 8 DB backends; (4) Koan.Jobs.TestKit (JobBehaviorSuite + JobsHarness) reused by 4 durable adapter suites (Mongo/Postgres/SqlServer/Sqlite) + Transport.Messaging. ARCH-0079 canon helper KoanIntegrationHost (tests/Shared/Koan.Testing/Integration/KoanIntegrationHost.cs): real IHost + AddKoan() reflective discovery, defaults to neutral 'Test' environment (load-bearing for the relational DDL guard); directly used in 8 dirs (Cache adapters/CrossEngine, Data.AI, Jobs core+TestKit, Integration/Bootstrap) and inherited by adapter suites via TestKits. Shared harness offers TestPipeline facade + container fixtures (Redis/Postgres/Mongo/Weaviate/OpenSearch/ElasticSearch/Couchbase, DockerDaemonFixture with env-var override → TCP ping → Testcontainers → docker CLI fallback; container-gated specs skip cleanly when infra is absent). Boot-smoke suite tests/Suites/Integration/Bootstrap has 14 pillar specs (AI, Auth×3, Cache, Canon, DataCore, Jobs, Mcp, Media, MessagingCore, Rag, Storage, Web). Husks/orphans exist: Suites/AI/Core/Koan.AI.Tests (GlobalUsings+testsuite.yaml, no csproj), Suites/Cache/Unit (only a stale TestResults.trx), Suites/Data/Vector/Koan.Tests.Data.Vector.Weaviate (one spec cs, no csproj).
coverageGaps: SOLUTION GAP (biggest finding): Koan.sln contains only 48 of the 87 test csproj on disk — 39 test projects are NOT in the sln, so `dotnet test Koan.sln` (the documented gate and green-ratchet leg A') never runs them. Missing from sln: ALL 7 remaining Data connector suites (Sqlite, Postgres, Mongo, Redis, InMemory, Json, PGVector — i.e. most FilterConvergence/ComparableEncoding oracle suites; only SqlServer + Couchbase are in), all 9 Web.AdapterSurface suites + TestKit, Data.AdapterSurface.TestKit, Data.Convergence.Tests, Data.Filtering.Tests, Media.Core.Tests, both Storage suites, all 3 Samples suites, Web.Admin/Web.Sort, Cache Analyzers/CrossEngine, Canon.Core.Tests, Context.Unit, Core.Tests(non-Unit), Data.AI, AI.Core.Tests, Koan.Web.Auth.Tests, S7.Meridian.Tests, S16.PantryPal.Tests, Mcp.TestHost

Messaging pillar: Koan.Messaging.Core has only a boot-smoke spec; the RabbitMq connector (src/Connectors/Messaging/RabbitMq) has ZERO integration tests (no Testcontainers broker anywhere in tests/) — a direct ARCH-0079 canon violation; Jobs.Transport.Messaging and Cache.Coherence.Messaging test their own layers over in-memory buses

NOTHING at all: Koan.Scheduling, Koan.Secrets.Core + Vault connector, Koan.Recipe.* , Koan.ServiceMesh, Koan.Data.Backup, Koan.Data.Cqrs, Koan.Data.Direct, Koan.Data.Lucene, Koan.Data.SearchEngine, Koan.Web.OpenApi, Koan.Web.Backup, Koan.Web.Auth.Roles, Koan.Web.Auth.Services, web auth connectors (Discord/Google/Microsoft/Oidc), Koan.AI.{Agents,Compute,Orchestration,Training,Web}, Koan.Admin, Koan.Flow.Core (legacy pre-Canon), Storage S3 connector (only Local is tested), GraphQl/Swagger web connectors, Service/Inbox connector

Orchestration (Cli, Cli.Core, Aspire, Generators): orchestration adapters are the canon's standing exemption, but the promised alternative integration tests (e.g. Compose-file shape assertions) do not exist; generators have no analyzer/generator tests except Koan.Cache.Analyzers
Unit-only pillars: Context (12 files), Tagging (5 files), and the thin loose Web projects (Json.Strict 1 file, Sse 1, PatchOps 2, Auth 3, WebSockets 4)
Samples coverage is health-check-only (per tests/README.md: '/health endpoint' for DocMind/PantryPal/McpService)
No coverage measurement gate anywhere: coverlet.collector referenced by some test csproj but no threshold, no report, no CI consumption
buildInfra: Koan.sln: 2,767 lines, 252 Project entries = 166 csproj + 86 solution folders (scripts/regenerate-sln.ps1 exists). TFM net10.0 repo-wide; global.json pins SDK 10.0.100 rollForward latestMinor. Versioning = Nerdbank.GitVersioning (ARCH-0085): root version.json {version: '0.17', versionHeightOffset: -1}, NBGV 3.9.50 injected via Directory.Build.props; Directory.Build.targets states ARCH-0082's versions.props/KoanPackageKind scheme is SUPERSEDED (KoanPackageKind survives as metadata only) and imports build/compat-ranges.targets (pack-time rewrite of Koan→Koan ProjectReference versions to bounded compat ranges). Root Directory.Build.props: PackageId prefix Sylin.* (code namespaces stay Koan.*), SourceLink GitHub + ContinuousIntegrationBuild=true default + EmbedUntrackedSources, snupkg symbols, Apache-2.0, GenerateDocumentationFile=true (NoWarn 1591), and two in-repo Roslyn generators attached as analyzers: Koan.Core.Registry.Generators (broad) and Koan.Orchestration.Generators (opt-in via KoanRequiresOrchestrationGenerator, set in src/Connectors/Directory.Build.props which re-imports the root props). build/ dir: compat-ranges.targets, kernel-manifest.txt, versions.props (deprecated remnant). packaging/: Koan.nuspec + Koan.App.nuspec meta-packages + README. local-feed/: 39 stale 0.8.2 nupkg/snupkg (live tree is 0.17.x — feed is 9 minor versions behind). scripts/ (~30 ps1): green-ratchet.ps1 (the merge gate), test-all.ps1 (kill-testhost → clean-test-binaries → build sln → dotnet test sln), docs-lint.ps1, validate-code-examples.ps1 (compiles C# blocks in instructional docs), validate-packages.ps1, pack-and-push.ps1, versioning/ (Update-Versions.ps1, Initialize-NbgvBaseline.ps1, New-Release.ps1, release-from-dev.ps1). tests/Directory.Build.props redirects all test bin/obj to %TEMP%\Koan-framework\tests\<project> and links SeedPacks as content. 11 samples ship start.bat (CLAUDE.md mandates start.bat over docker compose): preflight docker+dotnet checks, mkdir storage dirs, docker compose -p <project> with named compose file.
ci: Effectively NO CI. Of 7 workflows in .github/workflows, FIVE are disabled noop placeholders (ci.yml, validate-main-pr.yml, canary-nightly.yml, tag-on-main.yml, create-tag-on-main.yml — all renamed 'disabled-*', trigger workflow_dispatch only, single echo step) and nuget-release.yml is a disabled stub superseded by release-on-main.yml. The ONLY active workflow is release-on-main.yml (on: push to main): (1) dotnet build Koan.sln -c Release (with -p:NuGetAudit=false) — 'refuses to release a broken build'; (2) scripts/versioning/Update-Versions.ps1 -AutoBumpKernel writing build/versions.props; (3) commit+tag release/v<kernel>; (4) pack only bumped packages and push to nuget.org. It runs ZERO tests — its own comments call tests-only merges 'normal'. So: nothing gates PRs (no build, no tests, no lint on PR); integration tests NEVER run in CI; the entire quality gate is the local, voluntary scripts/green-ratchet.ps1 (legs: A build sln, A' dotnet test sln with container-gated skips, B docs-lint, C diff-scoped doc code-example compilation). Additional drift: the active release workflow still implements ARCH-0082 versions.props machinery that Directory.Build.props/targets declare superseded by NBGV (ARCH-0085) — the release path on GitHub is stale relative to the in-repo versioning canon (scripts/versioning/release-from-dev.ps1 and Initialize-NbgvBaseline.ps1 look like the newer path).
qualityGates: Repo-wide via root Directory.Build.props: Nullable=enable, ImplicitUsings=enable, LangVersion=latestMajor, GenerateDocumentationFile=true with NoWarn 1591 (missing-doc warnings suppressed), deterministic CI builds. TreatWarningsAsErrors=true ONLY for tests (tests/Directory.Build.props); src/ builds tolerate warnings. No Directory.Packages.props (no central package management — versions pinned per csproj, e.g. Koan.Core.csproj pins 30+ packages individually, with hand-written CVE-bump comments). .editorconfig is minimal and toothless: ~6 style prefs (file-scoped namespaces, var, using placement, crlf) all at :suggestion severity — zero warning/error severities, no dotnet_diagnostic rules, no AnalysisLevel/EnforceCodeStyleInBuild anywhere. No third-party analyzers (no StyleCop/Sonar/Roslynator). The 'analyzers' that exist are in-repo source generators (Registry, Orchestration) plus one shipped product analyzer (Koan.Cache.Analyzers, which does have its own test suite — though that suite is among the 39 projects missing from Koan.sln). Coverage: coverlet.collector sprinkled in some test csproj, no threshold or reporting gate. Net: quality enforcement is convention + the local green-ratchet script, not tooling.
observations: The test PLATFORM design is genuinely mature — ARCH-0079 'integration tests as canon' is codified in tests/README.md with a documented bug table proving its value (5 bug classes unit tests structurally missed), a single canon helper (KoanIntegrationHost) encoding hard-won environment semantics (Test env vs Production DDL guard), and 4 reusable TestKits giving N-adapters × 1-spec-battery leverage (FilterConvergence oracle is a textbook differential-testing design)

But the EXECUTION substrate contradicts it: with all CI disabled, the canon ('mandatory before release') is enforced only by the developer voluntarily running scripts/green-ratchet.ps1 locally — and releases to nuget.org happen on every main push gated by BUILD ONLY

The sln/disk drift (39/87 test projects outside Koan.sln, including most filter-oracle connector suites, both Storage suites, Media, and the Web AdapterSurface matrix) means even a faithful `dotnet test Koan.sln` ratchet run silently skips ~45% of test projects; either the sln was regenerated without them or they were never added — this should be the first fix (regenerate-sln.ps1 exists)

Three husk/orphan suite dirs (Suites/AI/Core, Suites/Cache/Unit, Suites/Data/Vector) and a checked-in TestResults.trx + .lscache files indicate incomplete migrations from the legacy tree (tests/README.md still references ../tests.old, which no longer exists)

Test weight tracks the redesign focus: Data (21 projects/158 files) and Web (13/93) dominate; the long tail of periphery pillars (Scheduling, Secrets, Recipe, ServiceMesh, Backup, Cqrs/Direct/Lucene/SearchEngine, AI periphery, auth connectors) has zero tests — consistent with a sole-implementor dogfooding strategy but a real risk for published Sylin.* packages

Versioning has 3 coexisting generations in-tree: deprecated build/versions.props (ARCH-0082), the ACTIVE release-on-main.yml still driving it, and NBGV version.json (ARCH-0085) declared canonical in Directory.Build.props — the GitHub release path and the in-repo canon disagree
Loose tests/ root projects (16) violate the suite contract the README declares; AI tests are split across both worlds (Suites/AI + 7 loose Koan.AI.*.Tests)

Local quality muscle is unusually strong for a no-CI repo: green-ratchet additionally compiles C# code blocks in instructional docs (validate-code-examples.ps1) and lints docs links/front-matter — gates most CI setups don't have

Repo hygiene nits: stray 'nul' files at repo root and tests/, malicious-project.json / test-project.json / query-*.csx experiment leftovers at root, PHASE1/PHASE2_IMPLEMENTATION_RESULTS.md scratch docs at root
